note: Playwright exit code was 0 (all batch runs completed), but 20 tests failed. FAIL verdict per 0-failure gate.

failures.test: alerts.spec.ts:165 — should display history section with triggers or empty message
failures.hypothesis: Test expects either ACK/NEW trigger entries OR 'No alert triggers yet' text. Likely timing issue with poll() or empty state rendering race.
failures.suggested_fix: Increase poll timeout or add waitForSelector for 'Recent Triggers' section to stabilize before checking content.

failures.test: api-tokens.spec.ts:17 — displays API Tokens section in settings
failures.hypothesis: Timeout waiting for 'Existing Tokens' / 'Create Token' / 'MCP Integration' text. Settings > API Tokens tab navigation failed or tab content not loading.
failures.suggested_fix: Verify /settings page loads Settings tabs nav. Check if 'API Tokens' tab exists and is clickable. May be missing from deployed build or feature-flagged off.

failures.test: api-tokens.spec.ts:23 — can create a new API token
failures.hypothesis: Same root cause as test :17 — cannot reach API Tokens section. Or Token name input field not found.
failures.suggested_fix: Same as :17. Verify API Tokens tab exists in deployed environment.

failures.test: api-tokens.spec.ts:34 — newly created token appears in the token list
failures.hypothesis: Same root cause as :17/:23.
failures.suggested_fix: Same as :17.

failures.test: api-tokens.spec.ts:50 — can revoke a token with confirmation
failures.hypothesis: Same root cause as :17/:23/:34.
failures.suggested_fix: Same as :17.

failures.test: byok-flow.spec.ts:108 — Settings BYOK test/save use backend response (not route unavailable)
failures.hypothesis: Known issue from flaky-registry.json: DB schema drift, user_ai_keys table missing created_at column. aiKeys.save returns 500.
failures.suggested_fix: Run DB migration to add created_at column to user_ai_keys table. Then re-enable test.
failures.related_flaky_registry_entry: byok-flow.spec.ts — Settings BYOK actions keep UX clear for saved key vs raw key

failures.test: confirm-dialog.spec.ts:12 — delete action shows confirmation dialog
failures.hypothesis: Timeout waiting for delete button (trash icon) on /settings/users. Test has `test.skip()` fallback if no delete button visible, but timeout occurred before skip could execute.
failures.suggested_fix: Reduce isVisible() timeout in test.skip() guard from default 30s to 5s. Or check if delete buttons are actually present in deployed environment (may be RBAC-hidden).

failures.test: confirm-dialog.spec.ts:27 — cancel does not delete the item
failures.hypothesis: Same root cause as :12.
failures.suggested_fix: Same as :12.

failures.test: confirm-dialog.spec.ts:42 — confirm deletes the item
failures.hypothesis: Same root cause as :12.
failures.suggested_fix: Same as :12.

failures.test: k-p2-features.spec.ts:60 — settings tabs: 7 tabs exist and can be switched
failures.hypothesis: Test expects exactly 7 settings tabs. Actual count may differ if API Tokens tab is missing or new tab was added.
failures.suggested_fix: Verify actual settings tab count in deployed environment. Update test to match, or fix missing tab in frontend.

failures.test: m-p3-features.spec.ts:57 — TimeRangeSelector renders with range options when Metrics tab is active
failures.hypothesis: Metrics tab click succeeded but TimeRangeSelector component (1h/6h/24h/7d buttons) did not render. Feature may be incomplete or behind feature flag.
failures.suggested_fix: Check if TimeRangeSelector component is rendered when Metrics tab is active on cluster detail page. May be missing from build.

failures.test: m-p3-features.spec.ts:74 — clicking a time range button updates selection
failures.hypothesis: Same root cause as :57 — TimeRangeSelector not rendering.
failures.suggested_fix: Same as :57.

failures.test: m-p3-features.spec.ts:175 — Customize button exists on dashboard (in widget mode)
failures.hypothesis: Dashboard Customize button is inside widgetMode guard. Test must click [data-testid='toggle-widget-mode-btn'] first. Timeout suggests toggle button or customize button not found.
failures.suggested_fix: Verify toggle-widget-mode-btn exists on dashboard. Check if widgetMode is enabled in deployed environment. May be feature-flagged off.

failures.test: m-p3-features.spec.ts:180 — clicking Customize shows DashboardEditBar
failures.hypothesis: Same root cause as :175.
failures.suggested_fix: Same as :175.

failures.test: optimistic-ui.spec.ts:9 — page navigation renders with transition
failures.hypothesis: Test timed out waiting for a[href='/deployments'] link. Link may not be visible in sidebar or renamed.
failures.suggested_fix: Verify /deployments link exists in sidebar navigation. Check if navigation item was renamed or removed.

failures.test: permissions.spec.ts:7 — admin can access /permissions page
failures.hypothesis: Timeout waiting for Permissions link in sidebar/settings nav. Link may not exist in deployed build or is RBAC-hidden even for admin.
failures.suggested_fix: Verify Permissions link exists in deployed frontend. Check if feature is behind feature flag or if route exists in backend.

failures.test: phase1-v194.spec.ts:173 — cluster row click navigates to /clusters/[id]
failures.hypothesis: Expected URL pattern /clusters/.+ but got /clusters (no id). Cluster row click does not navigate to detail page. This is BUG-RD-001 the test is designed to catch.
failures.suggested_fix: Fix cluster row onClick handler to navigate to /clusters/[id] instead of staying at /clusters. This is a known bug the test is validating.

failures.test: responsive.spec.ts:45 — mobile nav first tap should navigate immediately
failures.hypothesis: getByRole('link', { name: /^settings$/i }) resolved to 2 elements (strict mode violation). Both nav drawer and top bar have settings links.
failures.suggested_fix: Use .first() or filter by ancestor to select only the nav drawer settings link.

failures.test: responsive.spec.ts:58 — mobile nav tap lands on target route without login returnUrl drift
failures.hypothesis: Timeout waiting for events link in mobile nav. Link may not be visible after opening mobile nav drawer.
failures.suggested_fix: Increase timeout or add explicit wait for nav drawer animation to complete before clicking link.

failures.test: responsive.spec.ts:71 — should render BYOK actions as full-width touch targets on mobile
failures.hypothesis: Timeout waiting for 'AI Configuration' tab in settings. Tab may not exist or may be named differently in deployed build.
failures.suggested_fix: Verify 'AI Configuration' tab exists in /settings on deployed environment. Check if tab was renamed or removed.

patterns_detected: API Tokens tab (Settings) — 4 consecutive failures, all 30s timeouts → systemic. Tab likely missing from deployed build or feature-flagged off.
confirm-dialog.spec.ts — 3 failures, all 30s timeouts → delete buttons not found on /settings/users. RBAC or test skip guard issue.
responsive.spec.ts mobile nav — 3 failures → strict mode violations and timeouts. Mobile nav drawer selectors need refinement.
m-p3-features.spec.ts — 4 failures → TimeRangeSelector and Dashboard Customize features incomplete or disabled in deployed build.
Settings tabs count mismatch — expected 7, actual count differs (API Tokens tab missing?).
Permissions page — admin cannot access. Feature may be incomplete or behind feature flag.
recommendations: 1. API Tokens tab: Verify tab exists in deployed /settings page. If missing, add to frontend or mark tests as @skip with reason.
2. BYOK DB schema: Run migration to add created_at column to user_ai_keys table.
3. confirm-dialog tests: Reduce isVisible() timeout in skip guard from 30s to 5s to fail fast.
4. responsive.spec.ts: Fix strict mode violations by using .first() or filtering by ancestor.
5. m-p3-features TimeRangeSelector: Verify component is rendered on Metrics tab. If feature incomplete, skip tests.
6. Dashboard Customize: Verify toggle-widget-mode-btn exists and widgetMode is enabled.
7. Permissions page: Verify /permissions route and link exist in deployed build.
8. BUG-RD-001 (phase1-v194.spec.ts:173): Fix cluster row onClick to navigate to /clusters/[id].
